taxonomy: Diphyes rara is classified as a genus within the family Siphonophoridae, which is a group of colonial, siphonophores. The Siphonophoridae family is part of the phylum Cnidaria, which also includes jellyfish and corals. Cnidaria is a diverse group of marine animals, many of which are predators that capture prey using stinging cells called cnidocytes. The classification of Diphyes rara is further supported by its unique body structure, which is composed of many individual zooids working together to form a single organism. This colonial structure is a key characteristic that distinguishes Diphyes rara from other species within the Siphonophoridae family.
